description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a green fuselage and red upper and lower wings, featuring a classic double-wing structure supported by interplane struts and wires. The nose houses two prominent exposed piston engines, each with a wooden propeller, giving the aircraft a robust and mechanical appearance. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the engines, with a single seat and a small windshield for the pilot. The tail section is painted white with a green underside and displays a cross insignia on the vertical stabilizer, indicative of early 20th-century military aircraft. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The overall design is reminiscent of World War I-era fighter planes, with a compact, sturdy build and visually striking color contrasts.
description: A vintage green and red biplane with exposed twin piston engines, open cockpit, and fixed landing gear, reminiscent of early military aircraft.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin exposed engines, wooden propellers, open cockpit, and the full span of the double wings.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the green fuselage, red wings, and the white tail with a cross insignia.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, white vertical stabilizer with cross, and the structure of the biplane wings.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the cockpit position, engine detail, and the tail's green and white color split.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically and supported by interplane struts; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Twin exposed piston engines mounted on the fuselage sides, each with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers; features a cross insignia on the vertical fin.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the engines, single-seat with a small windshield.
